messages.subject: Newsletter
messages.summary: Welcome back from Spring Break! New nine weeks starting strong. Newsletter PDF attached with weekly schedule, subjects, and upcoming events.
messages.fullBody:
Hope you all had a great Spring Break! It's a new nine weeks and we are going to start strong- now that we are all rested!

I'm looking forward to making this last 9 weeks amazing!

Jeneen Cuccinello
Fourth Grade Teacher
Lake Whitney Elementary School
407-877-8888

attachments.extractedContent:
Week of: March 23-27, 2026

Upcoming Events:
• Monday — PE, Begin 4th grading period
• Tuesday — ART
  - ELA: Mixed Review of Reading Poetry and Prose and Reading Informational Text / Writing
  - Math: Data — MA.4.DP.1.1: Collect and represent numerical data, including fractional values, using tables, stem-and-leaf plots or line plots. MA.4.DP.1.2: Determine the mode, median or range. MA.4.DP.1.3: Solve real-world problems involving numerical data.
• Wednesday — PE, Early Release Day - Modified Schedule
  - Science: Heredity and Reproduction — SC.4.L.16.1 Identify processes of sexual reproduction in flowering plants. SC.4.L.16.2 Explain inherited vs environmental characteristics. SC.4.L.16.3 Recognize animal behaviors shaped by heredity and learning. SC.4.L.16.4 Compare/contrast life cycles of Florida plants and animals.
• Thursday — MUSIC
• Friday — PE
• Social Studies: Florida's Government

Reminders:
• It's a new 9 weeks — Start Strong!
• MARCH 31 — 4th grade BEST Writing Assessment
• APRIL 7 — PTO Spirit Night Fundraiser
• APRIL 8 — SAC/PTO 9:00/9:30
• APRIL 9 — ARTS Night
• APRIL 17-18 — School Play

messages.subject: Spring Break Weekly
messages.summary: Students averaged 98% on science test and 90% on word study quiz! Newsletter attached with March 23-27 schedule, daily math HW, and upcoming events.
messages.fullBody:
I hope everyone is enjoying an excellent spring break! Attached is the newsletter and pic of last week❤️ Speaking of last week…the students averaged a 98% on their science test and a 90% on their word study quiz! Way to go class!

Have a wonderful rest of your break!

Ms. Smith
Adrienne.Smith@ocps.net

messages.subject: Writing Test
messages.summary: Information about the BEST Writing Assessment. PDF attached with official OCPS letter about the March 31 state test.
messages.fullBody:
Please read the attached information about BEST Writing.

Jeneen Cuccinello
Fourth Grade Teacher
Lake Whitney Elementary School
407-877-8888

attachments.description: Official OCPS letter about BEST Writing Assessment
attachments.extractedContent:
March 4, 2026

Dear Parent/Guardian,

Your student will take the computer-based Grade 4 Benchmarks for Excellent Student Thinking (B.E.S.T.) Writing assessment on Tuesday, March 31, 2026. The B.E.S.T. Writing assessment is administered in one 120-minute session, and students may work up to half of a typical school day.

You or your student can review the computer-based sample items and certain released tests at home at https://flfast.org/families.html.

Key policies:
• Electronic Devices — Students are not allowed to have any electronic devices, including cell phones, smart glasses, and smartwatches, at any time during testing or during breaks, even if the devices are turned off.
• Testing Rules Acknowledgment — Students click a box to acknowledge testing rules before beginning.
• Discussing Test Content — Students may not share details about test items after testing (no texting, emailing, or posting to social media). They may talk about their testing experience with parents/families.
• Working Independently — Students must do their own work and protect answers from being seen.
• Leaving Campus — If a student leaves before completing a session, they cannot return to that session.
• Extra Time — Students still working at end of allotted time will be provided extra time.

If you have questions, contact Eileen Warder at (407)877-8888 ext. 3534224.
For more info: https://flfast.org/fsa.html

Sincerely,
Dr. Crabb

messages.fullBody:
Hi Parents!
   We had an amazing day! We are running late due to a change in drivers. It looks like we may be about 30 minutes late. I will send another message when we are about 30 minutes from the school.
Thank you for your understanding!

[Follow-up at 6:38 PM]: We are about 40 minutes out!

messages.subject: Field Day
messages.summary: Field Day details for March 12. ADDitions volunteer sign-up required to attend on campus.
messages.fullBody:
Dear Parents,
Field Day will take place on March 12, 2026, and will look a little different this year. During their scheduled special area times, students will rotate through a variety of activity stations.

If you would like to attend on campus that day, you will need to be an ADDitions volunteer and sign up to assist at one of the stations. Please see the sign up opportunities in Parent Square.

If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out. Thank you for your support — we appreciate it!

Coach Goodwin
Tracy Goodwin
PE Teacher
Lake Whitney Elementary

messages.subject: Weekly
messages.summary: Happy Sunday Funday! Numeracy dress-up week ahead with assessments all week. Newsletter and class photo attached.
messages.fullBody:
Happy Sunday Funday!
Enjoy a restful day as we head into a strongly assessed "Numeracy" dress up week:

• Favorite Number/Marvelous Math Monday: Foundational Phonics – Diphthongs ou, ow, oi, oy, au, aw
• Pattern Power Tuesday: Native American Culture and Science Quizzes
• Wacky Fraction Wednesday: Math Quiz and Cold Read Comprehension Test – Learning Checks will be sent home Tuesday for question review.
• Crazy Hair/Outfit Field Day Thursday: Math and Science Finals

I have time to help students wrap up Quarter 3 work this week. If your child has anything missing, please let me know and we can work together to get it completed.

Have a wonderful evening!
Ms. Smith

attachments.extractedContent:
March 9-13, 2026

Phonics Focus: Concept of Diphthong — ou, ow, oi, oy, au, aw

Our Learning:
• ELA: Assessing similes, idioms, alliteration in text, rhyme schemes & themes in poems. Identify and use context clues, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine meaning of unknown words.
• Math: Compare Numbers Using Place Value.
• Science: Identify human body parts and their basic functions for living.
• Social Studies: Review to Assess How Can We Learn About Native American Cultures? Quiz

messages.subject: field day reminder
messages.summary: Field Day will be different this year — volunteers only at stations, no walk-around observers. Sign up required.
messages.fullBody:
Hello,
Field Day will be different this year. Instead of a timeslot with multiple grade levels we will be going with only 2nd grade during a switched special area time. In the years past parents have been able to walk around and watch their child, but this year will be volunteers only. What that means is that if you want to attend field day you will have to be ADDitions approved and sign up for one of her volunteer stations. You will be staying at the station the whole time instead of rotating with the class. Flyer attached where you can sign up.

Ms. Smith

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Wishing You a Safe and Restful Spring Break
schoolDistrictComms.summary: Thank you for the important role you play each day in supporting your child's education. As we begin spring break, I want to wish you and your family a safe, happy, and relaxing week. When students return to school on Monday, March 23, we will begin the final nine weeks of the school year — an exciting time filled with important learning experiences, milestones, and celebrations.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Field Day Recap & Reminders
schoolDistrictComms.summary: Field Day was held on March 12 with fun activities, inflatables, and a DJ. Only pre-registered volunteers were permitted on campus for security. Thank you to all parents who signed up to help make it a success!

schoolDistrictComms.subject: 15th Annual DEF Silent Auction & Gala — April 18
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The 15th Annual DEF Silent Auction & Gala is Saturday, April 18 from 6-10 PM at the West Orange Country Club. Theme: Mardi Gras Masquerade. Tickets are $50/person (includes dinner, 2 drink tickets, and auction access). All funds go to the Dolphin Education Fund for classroom supplies, arts programs, PE equipment, and more. Purchase tickets at tinyurl.com/DEF-Gala.

schoolDistrictComms.from: Foundation for Orange County Public Schools
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The Foundation for OCPS is asking families to take the $1 Pledge at the Give Education Day website. Funds support literacy enrichment, STEM and arts access, college and career readiness, scholarships, and professional development for teachers.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Mondays with Maria — Weekly Superintendent Update
schoolDistrictComms.summary: This week's Mondays with Maria featured a visit to Colonial High School with new Deputy Superintendent Jose Martinez, who previously served as the school's principal. Colonial High students shared what they love most about their campus. Available as a video and podcast.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Dolphin Digest — Week of March 2, 2026
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The principal's weekly update highlighted an incredibly busy and rewarding week including All-County Music performances and neon dance activities. Students continue to shine in everything they do. The newsletter also covered upcoming events and school community updates.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Yearbook Price Increase — Order Before March 1
schoolDistrictComms.summary: Yearbook prices increased after March 1, 2026. Families were encouraged to order before the deadline to get the lower price. Yearbooks capture all the memories from this school year.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: INFORM: OCPS — District 4 News: Black History Month 2026
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The District 4 newsletter covered Black History Month recognition events and second semester updates across Orange County Public Schools. The newsletter highlighted community celebrations and student achievements throughout the district.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Coming Soon: New Online Digital Enrollment Platform
schoolDistrictComms.summary: OCPS is launching a new online digital enrollment platform. No action required at this time.

assignments.subject: Writing
assignments.title: BEST Writing Practice Essay
assignments.description: Practice persuasive writing prompt — 45 minutes timed. Prepare for the March 31 state assessment.

assignments.subject: Reading
assignments.title: Reading Log — 20 min/night
assignments.description: Read independently and log pages in reading journal. Check Canvas and Skyward for any missing assignments from Q3.

assignments.subject: Phonics
assignments.title: Soft 'c' /s/ Word Study (_ce pattern)
assignments.description: Practice Soft 'c' /s/ words using the _ce pattern (e.g., face, race, place, space). Potential Word Study Quiz on Friday.

assignments.subject: Math
assignments.title: Daily Math HW — Pages 153-162
assignments.description: Mon: Lesson 11-1 pgs 153-154. Tue: Lesson 11-2 pgs 155-156. Wed: Lesson 11-3 pgs 157-158. Thu: Lesson 11-5 pgs 161-162. Add/Subtract with Numbers to 1,000 using place-value strategies.

assignments.subject: ELA
assignments.title: Money Madness — Open Book Test Friday
assignments.description: Review Money Madness for Friday's open book test. Access from OCPS Launchpad > Dashboard > My Backpack Apps > McGrawHill > Grade 2 Wonders FL > Resources > Literature Collection > pgs 490-509.

assignments.subject: Math
assignments.title: Blooket: Extend Thinking of Numbers to 1,000
assignments.description: Complete the Blooket math homework game assigned by Ms. Smith. Practice extending thinking of numbers to 1,000.

assignments.subject: Science
assignments.title: Blooket: Human Body Parts Review
assignments.description: Complete both Blooket science review games assigned by Ms. Smith. Study human body parts and their basic functions.

assignments.subject: Reading
assignments.title: Reading Log — 15 min/night
assignments.description: Read independently and have a parent sign the log

assignments.subject: Social Studies
assignments.title: Social Studies Review Game — Native American Cultures
assignments.description: Review game shared by Ms. Smith for studying Native American Cultures. Play on Educaplay.

highlights.title: Community Dance Recap
highlights.body: Thank you for your wonderful support at our school dance on February 27 — it was a joy to see our community come together.

highlights.title: Student Spotlight: Black History Month
highlights.body: Haven Bennett was awarded 1st place at the Black History Essay Contest for West Orange County, representing Lake Whitney!

highlights.title: Book Fair Success
highlights.body: The Scholastic Book Fair was a huge success! Thank you to Mrs. Dudek and all the volunteers who made it possible.

highlights.title: Field Day — March 12
highlights.body: Field Day is approaching! All guests must be ADDitions-approved volunteers to attend. Ensure your clearance is active and sign up for a volunteer shift.

highlights.title: School of Excellence
highlights.body: Lake Whitney has been recognized as a 2024-25 School of Excellence by the Florida Department of Education!

midWeekCheck.summary: New newsletter from Ms. Cuccinello (March 23-27) and an Amazon wishlist request for classroom room transformation. No new emails from Ms. Smith. OCPS digest announced new digital enrollment platform (no action required).

actionItems.title: Prepare for BEST Writing Assessment
actionItems.description: This is a 120-minute Florida State Test. Ensure Bronson gets a good night's sleep Monday and a healthy breakfast Tuesday. No electronic devices are allowed at school.

actionItems.description: The 3rd grading period report cards will be available on Skyward.
